units.content: The code is a thin wrapper over GraphEvent history that structures append-only events into typed timeline entries
The code performs pure reads with no state, and history is passed explicitly
The summarize_event function generates terse human-readable one-liners for GraphEvent objects
The function degrades gracefully when event details are missing
The code is designed to handle different event types (coupling-added, convergence-changed) with specific formatting for each
The _event_mentions_entity function checks if an event is relevant to a given entity by checking measurerId, targetId, or actor fields
The get_entity_timeline function builds a timeline for an entity appearing as measurer, target, or actor
Events in the timeline are sorted by timestamp and ID
The EntityTimeline object includes metadata about the timeline such as total_events, first_event, last_event, and distinct_actors
The get_coupling_key_timeline function creates a timeline for a specific coupling key showing its full overwrite history
